content:
Actually, that’s a common misconception. As an AI developed by OpenAI, I **do not have real-time access to the internet** or the ability to browse websites when answering your questions. My responses are generated based on a mixture of:

- **A large dataset**: I was trained on a wide range of information, including books, articles, websites, and other text sources up to my knowledge cutoff date (June 2024).
- **Pattern recognition**: I use statistical patterns in that training data to generate relevant, accurate, and helpful answers to your queries.

**Why do people think AI has internet access?**  
Some people assume AI has live internet access because my responses can be quite up-to-date and detailed, giving the impression I’m “looking things up.” However, everything I provide comes from the information I was trained on—nothing is fetched live from the web.

**Are there exceptions?**  
Some AI tools or chatbots, in specific configurations, can be connected to the internet for live search or plugin functionality, but the base version of ChatGPT (what you’re using here) does not do this unless explicitly enabled.

**Summary:**  
I generate answers based on learned data, not by searching the internet in real time. If you have questions about recent events or need real-time data, I may not have the latest details. Always double-check important or time-sensitive information!
